Applied Arts of Europe

Vase (Vase étrusque à rouleau)
Sèvres Porcelain Manufactory (1756–present) Model designed by Charles Percier (born France, 1764–1838) Decoration designed by Alexandre-Theodore Brogniart (born France, 1739–1813) Flowers and ornament painted by Gilbert Drouet (active France, 1785–1825) Birds painted by Christophe-Ferdinand Caron (active France, 1792–1815) Sèvres, France
Oil or Vinegar Cruet
Meissen Porcelain Manufactory (1710–present) Model by Johann Joachim Kändler (born Saxony [now Germany], 1706–1775) Meissen, Electorate of Saxony (now Germany)
Elephant Candelabrum Vase (Vase à Tête d'Eléphant)
Sèvres Porcelain Manufactory (French, founded 1740) Design attributed to Jean-Claude Duplessis (French, c. 1695–1774) Painted by Pierre-Louis-Philippe Armand (French, active 1758–1781)
Tureen in the form of a Rabbit
Chelsea Porcelain Factory (c. 1744–84) London
Vase d'Arezzo
Sèvres Porcelain Manufactory Sèvres, France, founded 1740 Designed by Albert-Ernest Carrier-Belleuse (French, 1824-1887) Decorated by Henri Lucien Lambert (French, 1836-1909)
Portland Vase
Wedgwood Manufactory (1759–present) Etruria, Staffordshire, England
Salver presented to the Archduchess Maria Theresa of Austria by King Louis XV of France, 1757
Sèvres Porcelain Manufactory (1756–present) Sèvres, France
Dish
China
Moon Flask
Minton (Stoke-on-Trent, England, founded 1793)
Soup Plate presented to the Conde d’Aranda, Spanish ambassador to the French court, by King Louis XVI of France
Sèvres Porcelain Manufactory (1756–present) Painted by François-Marie Barrat (French, active 1769–91, 1795–96) Gilded by Andre-Joseph La France (French, active 1776–1803, 1813–28) Sèvres, France
Vase
Meissen Porcelain Manufactory (German, founded 1710) Model probably by Johann Jakob Irminger (1635–1724, born Switzerland, active Saxony) Meissen, Saxony, Germany
Portions of a Field Armor
Jacob Halder (English, 1558–1608) Royal Workshops of Greenwich, England
